jokes.text: Earthquake!
jokes.setup: Someone appears to be experiencing trembling or shaking
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be a dance move, not an actual earthquake
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that works well in the moment but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Exceptional buns, Geoffrey. - Thank you, sir. Yes, I think he's been doing the StairMaster.
jokes.setup: Phil compliments Geoffrey on his 'buns' (bread)
jokes.punchline: Will misinterprets it as Phil commenting on Geoffrey's buttocks from exercise
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre that plays on Will's tendency to misinterpret situations

jokes.text: Or 25 days ago, for that matter.
jokes.setup: Phil says he wouldn't expect to fit into pants from 25 years ago
jokes.punchline: Will adds that Phil probably couldn't fit into pants from 25 days ago either
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and timing, roasting Phil's weight in character for Will

jokes.text: I wish I could find a guy to lie to me like that.
jokes.setup: Phil romantically says Vivian is as beautiful as the day he married her
jokes.punchline: Hilary cynically calls it lying but says she wants that kind of lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Hilary's shallow, romantic yet cynical character - wants romance but acknowledges the deception

jokes.text: G, how's he supposed to remember what he had for dinner 25 years ago?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if they should prepare the same meal from 25 years ago
jokes.punchline: Will points out the impossibility of remembering a meal from so long ago
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bigger joke - reasonable observation that makes Phil's detailed memory more surprising

jokes.text: Well, we started out with some chicken pot pie... and some delicious coleslaw. And then there was some really, really great baked beans, with, what, molasses. And for dessert, we had a big huge chunk of chocolate devil's food cake.
jokes.setup: Will just said it's impossible to remember a 25-year-old meal
jokes.punchline: Phil proceeds to remember it in elaborate detail
jokes.explanation: Phil's obsession with food manifested perfectly - the detail escalates the absurdity

jokes.text: I'm guessing. If only that power could be used for good.
jokes.setup: Phil's incredibly detailed memory of the wedding meal
jokes.punchline: Phil admits he was guessing, Will jokes about his 'power' being wasted
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - the 'power' reference is both specific and universally relatable, great economy

jokes.text: Maybe I'll go upstairs to try to cheer up your mother. I'm so fat! On second thought, I'll call her from the car.
jokes.setup: Phil decides to go comfort his dieting wife
jokes.punchline: Immediately hears her yell about being fat and retreats
jokes.explanation: Good timing and cowardly husband trope executed well with Phil's character

jokes.text: Look, here's me at the beach. And here's me in my carriage. Look, and here's me at the park.
jokes.setup: Carlton showing family photos for anniversary album
jokes.punchline: All the photos are just of Carlton in different locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton narcissism - thinks a family album should feature primarily him

jokes.text: I knew we should have gotten them those Ginsu knives.
jokes.setup: Carlton's boring, self-centered photo album
jokes.punchline: Ashley dryly suggests they should have just bought infomercial knives instead
jokes.explanation: Good '90s reference and Ashley's practical wisdom contrasted with Carlton's vanity

jokes.text: Well, that's right, y'all, and that's why I came out here... 'cause you people really need help.
jokes.setup: Carlton says nothing exciting happens to their family
jokes.punchline: Will breaks fourth wall, acknowledging he's there to make the show/family more interesting
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor that acknowledges the show's premise while staying in character

jokes.text: Carlton Banks is wearing an elegant, yet masculine cotton-silk combination... designed by Will St. LeCarlton.
jokes.setup: Will doing a fashion show commentary
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous designer name combining Will and Carlton's names
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly predictable fashion parody setup

jokes.text: Say... is that Philip Banks or Blair Underwood?
jokes.setup: Phil presumably looking good in new clothes
jokes.punchline: Will compares Phil to attractive actor Blair Underwood
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference compliment, works for the era but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I look like Little Richard, attorney at law.
jokes.setup: Phil trying on what's presumably a flashy outfit
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to Little Richard but adds his profession
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of Little Richard's flamboyance with Phil's staid profession - memorable phrase

jokes.text: A-wop-bop-a-loo-bop, a-lop-bam-boom.
jokes.setup: Following up on the Little Richard comparison
jokes.punchline: Philip actually performs Little Richard's signature vocal style
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Philip doesn't just reference Little Richard, he commits to the bit. Great character moment showing his playful side.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, give it up for the star of Heidi Comes To Harlem.
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Ashley) in a dress that looks like traditional German/Alpine clothing
jokes.punchline: Will creates mashup title combining Heidi with Harlem location
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural mashup that plays on incongruity between European innocence and urban setting

jokes.text: Mom, if you make me go to school like this... I'm going to write a book when I grow up.
jokes.setup: Ashley in embarrassing outfit
jokes.punchline: Threatens to expose family in future tell-all book
jokes.explanation: Smart threat that's both age-appropriate and genuinely threatening to parents - good Ashley sass

jokes.text: Welcome to the Briny Deep... where none of our prices are ever too steep. We've got fresh fish, you can look in our tank. To get to the bathroom, make a left at the plank. Follow me.
jokes.setup: Hilary working as a restaurant hostess
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate rhyming spiel for a seafood restaurant
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's tendency to overcomplicate simple tasks with theatrical flair

jokes.text: Will Smith is the scum of the earth.
jokes.setup: Hilary appears to be acting strangely
jokes.punchline: She insults Will Smith (the actor) by name
jokes.explanation: Breaking fourth wall by having character insult the actual actor is bold but somewhat jarring

jokes.text: Your Montrose vase... attracts dust like a magnet!
jokes.setup: Family trying to understand Hilary's strange behavior
jokes.punchline: She makes dramatic accusation about vase attracting dust
jokes.explanation: Absurd complaint that sounds dramatic but is completely mundane - fits pattern

jokes.text: Philip, I have $1,000 with your name on it. Do the family a favor, get Viv some cooking lessons.
jokes.setup: Jazz offering money to Philip
jokes.punchline: Wants him to get Vivian cooking lessons (implying her cooking is terrible)
jokes.explanation: Standard bad cooking joke with Jazz's typical inappropriate timing

jokes.text: Vroom, vroom? Very clever. I'll get you two. Vroom, vroom, vroom.
jokes.setup: Jazz asks how a Mercedes sounds
jokes.punchline: Ashley makes car sounds, Jazz says he'll get her two more cars
jokes.explanation: Simple sound effect wordplay that escalates appropriately but predictably

jokes.text: I quit!
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to bribe family members and acting like he owns the place
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey finally reaches breaking point and quits
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to building tension - Geoffrey's dignity has limits even as hired help

jokes.text: How come they just couldn't give him some cereal?
jokes.setup: The Trix rabbit revelation
jokes.punchline: Will's genuine childhood confusion about why rabbit can't have cereal
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that maintains the childlike perspective and adds genuine pathos

jokes.text: And if you put me on the Bench, I'll take a real bite out of crime. Bite. Bite. Bite.
jokes.setup: Phil's campaign video for judge
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bite' pun while apparently literally biting something on screen
jokes.explanation: Predictable food pun for Phil but the visual repetition adds some absurdity

jokes.text: Kill him? I want to know the name of his decorator.
jokes.setup: Will wants to kill Carlton for embarrassing campaign video
jokes.punchline: Hilary is impressed by Carlton's room decor instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response - completely misses the point, focuses on shallow aesthetic concerns

jokes.text: Look, a Donna Karan suit, right in the middle of the kitchen floor. What color? You gotta come out to see that.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Vivian out of her room by mentioning expensive designer clothing
jokes.punchline: Vivian's only interested in the color, won't come out for any other reason
jokes.explanation: Shows Vivian's fashion consciousness even while in crisis and Will's manipulation tactics

jokes.text: I think it's time you came out of the closet.
jokes.setup: Will talking to Vivian who's hiding in her room
jokes.punchline: Literal 'closet' but sounds like the coming-out expression
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre that works in context but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Will, check out the talent. She's cool. She's hot. She's... your baby sister, man!
jokes.setup: Carlton admiring an attractive girl
jokes.punchline: Will reveals it's Ashley, Carlton's cousin
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward family situation - Ashley growing up creates uncomfortable moments

jokes.text: Hey, I didn't know Mom was a ventriloquist. Look at that funny-looking little dummy on her knee.
jokes.setup: Looking at old family photos
jokes.punchline: Will calls baby Carlton a ventriloquist dummy
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that plays on Carlton's unusual appearance and mannerisms even as baby

jokes.text: Ashley, that's Carlton.
jokes.setup: Will's ventriloquist dummy comment
jokes.punchline: Ashley matter-of-factly identifies it as Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that makes the insult even funnier by treating it as factual

jokes.text: So, where shall we scatter the ashes?
jokes.setup: Phil panicking about Vivian killing him for losing wedding ring
jokes.punchline: Will jumps to discussing Phil's funeral arrangements
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation that's perfectly timed and economical - assumes Phil is doomed

jokes.text: I was eating. That narrows it down.
jokes.setup: Trying to remember when Phil lost his ring
jokes.punchline: Phil says he was eating, Will sarcastically notes that doesn't help
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-based humor - Phil eats so constantly that it's not helpful information

jokes.text: I'm guessing the bride won't be wearing white.
jokes.setup: Jazz commenting on the wedding renewal
jokes.punchline: Implies Vivian isn't pure/virginal anymore
jokes.explanation: Typical Jazz inappropriate comment but fairly predictable white dress joke

jokes.text: I wouldn't draw you a cartoon.
jokes.setup: Jazz asks Hilary to draw him a bath
jokes.punchline: Hilary refuses to draw him anything, including a cartoon
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that shows Hilary's attitude toward Jazz - complete dismissal with style

jokes.text: My family. Can't live with 'em, can't turn your back on 'em.
jokes.setup: Family scrambling for reward money
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'can't live with them, can't live without them' saying
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar saying that captures the family's opportunistic behavior

jokes.text: Well, Bob, let's back it up for a moment and define this thing called friendship. You see, the ancient Mayans...
jokes.setup: Game show asking about Jazz's best friend
jokes.punchline: Carlton launches into overly academic response about ancient civilizations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - turns simple question into academic dissertation, completely missing the point

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Bob, this is a bad time.
jokes.setup: Game show asking Jazz who his best friend is
jokes.punchline: Jazz awkwardly deflects rather than answer
jokes.explanation: Jazz's social awkwardness showing - can't admit friendship feelings on TV

jokes.text: You gotta act like you got this tic, right? Like the army did this experiment on you, right, that just went terribly wrong. You're like, back up! Back up! Mind your business, that's all. Mind your business.
jokes.setup: Will giving advice on psychological warfare
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates fake military trauma tic with exaggerated paranoid behavior
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart survival tactics showing through in absurd military trauma performance

jokes.text: I work all day trying to keep this house nice for you. I cook and clean and work my fingers to the bone. And this is the thanks I get? Well, I bust my hump at school all day... and you just sit around here watching soap operas and eating bonbons.
jokes.setup: Ashley and Carlton apparently role-playing as married couple
jokes.punchline: They deliver classic married couple argument clichés
jokes.explanation: Standard domestic argument tropes - familiar but not particularly fresh execution

jokes.text: Wait a minute. We sound like an old married couple. So now I'm old.
jokes.setup: Ashley realizes they're acting like married couple
jokes.punchline: Carlton takes offense at the 'old' part specifically
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Carlton's vanity makes him focus on wrong part of observation

jokes.text: And I gave you the best years of my life.
jokes.setup: Continuing the married couple role-play after breaking character
jokes.punchline: Carlton continues the domestic drama despite the meta moment
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the bit even after breaking the fourth wall

jokes.text: No, you ate them all without offering me one. Can't have my toothpick, either. Did I ask you?
jokes.setup: Carlton and Ashley continuing their trapped/married dynamic
jokes.punchline: Petty arguments over breath mints and toothpicks
jokes.explanation: Realistic petty couple behavior but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: What's the magic word? Now. Guess again.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks for magic word to open something
jokes.punchline: Ashley says 'Now' instead of 'Please', gets rejected
jokes.explanation: Simple power play between trapped characters - Ashley's impatience vs Carlton's passive aggression

jokes.text: Come on, Uncle Phil, it didn't work on your wedding night... and it ain't going to work now.
jokes.setup: Phil says he'll fake a heart attack during the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Will reveals Phil tried the same trick on his actual wedding night
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor revealing Phil's cowardly history - implies he tried to avoid wedding night intimacy

jokes.text: Everybody, duck. I think she's about to blow.
jokes.setup: Vivian in tight wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Will warns everyone she might burst out of the dress
jokes.explanation: Standard tight clothing joke with energetic delivery but predictable setup

jokes.text: It's just, always a bridesmaid, never a bride.
jokes.setup: Jazz appearing emotional at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Uses classic female wedding complaint despite being male
jokes.explanation: Good gender role reversal humor - Jazz expressing traditionally feminine wedding envy

jokes.text: No, I don't. I take her for much more than that. For my lover... my life companion... my very best friend.
jokes.setup: Phil appears to reject Vivian at the altar
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually giving more elaborate, romantic vows
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that creates genuine tension before sweet romantic payoff - good structure

jokes.text: Thanks, kids, but... Look, Vivian, there's something I have to tell you. I lost my wedding ring. You mean this one? You found it! Yeah, I took it to have it inscribed. See, look. 'To a hundred years more.'
jokes.setup: Phil confessing he lost the ring at the altar
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals she had it inscribed as a surprise
jokes.explanation: Sweet resolution that ties together the ring plot - romantic rather than purely comedic but satisfying
